LEARNING TO ANIMATE IMAGES VIA LATENT SPACE NAVIGATION | section=### 3.4 INFERENCE | kind=paragraph $$ \begin{equation*} z_{s \rightarrow t}=z_{s \rightarrow r}+w_{r \rightarrow t}, t \in\{1, \ldots, T\} \tag{11} \end{equation*} $$ [DOC3_C076] (score=0.1673) meta: doc=DOC3:LATENT IMAGE ANIMATOR:
LEARNING TO ANIMATE IMAGES VIA LATENT SPACE NAVIGATION | owner=paper:LATENT IMAGE ANIMATOR:
LEARNING TO ANIMATE IMAGES VIA LATENT SPACE NAVIGATION | section=### 4.3 ABLATION STUDY | kind=paragraph (e) How many directions are required in $D_{m}$ ? Towards finding an effective size of $D_{m}$, we empirically test three different $M$, viz. 5, 10, 20, 40 and 100. Quantitative results in Tab. 5 show that when using 20 directions, the model achieves the best reconstruction results. ### 4.4 FURTHER ANALYSIS [DOC3_C078] (score=0.1754) meta: doc=DOC3:LATENT IMAGE ANIMATOR:
LEARNING TO ANIMATE IMAGES VIA LATENT SPACE NAVIGATION | owner=paper:LATENT IMAGE ANIMATOR:
LEARNING TO ANIMATE IMAGES VIA LATENT SPACE NAVIGATION | section=### 4.4 FURTHER ANALYSIS | kind=figure | role=citation_context Figure 5: Visualization of reference images. Example source (top) and reference images (down) from VoxCeleb, TaichiHD and TED-talk datasets. Our network learns reference images of a consistently frontal pose, systematically for all input images of each dataset. ![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/2024_06_04_9e3a30715f3e4c89014ag-09.jpg?height=368&width=1358&top_left_y=909&top_left_x=381) [DOC3_C080] (score=0.1682) meta: doc=DOC3:LATENT IMAGE ANIMATOR:
LEARNING TO ANIMATE IMAGES VIA LATENT SPACE NAVIGATION | owner=paper:LATENT IMAGE ANIMATOR:
LEARNING TO ANIMATE IMAGES VIA LATENT SPACE NAVIGATION | section=### 4.4 FURTHER ANALYSIS | kind=paragraph | role=citation_context reference images can be considered as a normalized form of $x_{s}$, learning transformations between $x_{s}$ and $x_{d}$ using $x_{s} \rightarrow x_{r} \rightarrow x_{d}$ is considerably more efficient than $x_{s} \rightarrow x_{d}$, once $x_{r}$ is fixed. [DOC3_C085] (score=0.1666) meta: doc=DOC3:LATENT IMAGE ANIMATOR:
